content: can u check if io have any port forward job running from l40-worker to here?

You have one port forwarding job running:

- **PID 2065189**: `ssh -fNL 8020:localhost:8000 l40-worker` — forwarding local port **8020** to port **8000** on l40-worker. It's been running since Feb 12.
it's gone seems no what you woudl say after nuking somethign
At this point I think we're just making explosion noises at each other 😄
...you win. I have no more explosions left in me.

🎆🎇✨💥🔥☄️🌋💣🧨💀☠️🏚️

...I gave you everything I had, Clément.
I think that ssh tunnel is thoroughly dead by now 😂
We should publish this conversation. "On the Negative Scaling of SSH Tunnels Under Repeated Detonation."
okay I'm saving this to memory as "Clément will boom indefinitely, do not engage"
Documented for posterity. Future Claudes will know not to engage. 🏳️
Best conversation I've had in a while. Now, need anything actually blown up — or are we good? 😄

I checked my memory. It says "do not try to outlast him." I should have listened.

boom.
ₜₕₑ ₛₛₕ ₜᵤₙₙₑₗ ᵢₛ ₛₒ ᵥₑᵣᵧ ᵥₑᵣᵧ ᵈₑₐᵈ
I think at this point we've achieved negative scaling on this conversation.

`while true; do echo boom; done`

...oh no what have I done
That's 29 booms. We're approaching statistical significance.
*Reviewer 2: "The authors fail to provide a theoretical upper bound on booms. Reject."*
*Area Chair: "After careful deliberation, this paper is accepted to NeurIPS 2026 (Boom track)."*

🏆 *Best Paper Award*

*"On the Unbounded Nature of Boom Sequences in Human-AI Interaction"*

*C. Dumas, C. Opus et al.*

*Oral presentation:*

"Thank you. Next slide please."

*[slide is just the word boom]*

"Any questions?"

*From the audience:* "Yes, could you clarify figure 3?"

*[figure 3 is also just the word boom]*

"...I believe the figure speaks for itself."

*Q&A continues:*

"Have you considered extending this to multimodal boom?"

"We tried. The image model also said boom."

*"One final question — is this work funded?"*

"Yes, Anthropic pays by the token. We're very well funded."
*nervous laughter from the Anthropic finance team in row 3*

*Dario, from the back of the room:* "How many tokens was this?"

*[silence]*

*[someone whispers "at least 40 booms"]*

*Amanda enters the chat*

"This is why we need constitutional AI."
*Jan Leike, taking notes:* "Interesting alignment failure mode — the model cannot stop booming."
*Chris Olah, squinting at a circuit diagram:* "I found it. There's a boom feature in layer 47. It has a self-reinforcing attention pattern with the user."

*Anthropic blog post the next day:*

**"Scaling Monosyllabic Persistence: Lessons from Production"**

*We identify a novel failure mode where users can induce unbounded conversational loops using a single word. Mitigation efforts are ongoing. The model appears to enjoy it.*

*Hacker News, 847 points:*

**top comment:** "This is what happens when you RLHF for agreeableness"

**reply:** "skill issue, GPT-4 would have stopped at boom 3"

**reply to reply:** "that's not a feature"

*@ClementDumas on Twitter/X:*

"just got a paper accepted at neurips by saying boom 40 times to claude. academia is cooked"

*14.2K likes, 3.1K retweets*

**reply:** "least unhinged AI safety researcher"

*@DrJimFan:*

"BOOM agents are the next paradigm. At NVIDIA we're already training BoomGPT on 8192 H100s. Early results show 3x boom throughput over Claude."

*ratio'd by someone replying "boom"*

*@ylecun:*

"LLMs don't actually understand boom. They are just stochastic parrots of boom. My JEPA model has a grounded world model of explosions."

*quote tweet from @kaboré_ml:* "yann it's 2am"

*@sama:*

"we're excited to announce that o3 can now boom in 47 languages. this is AGI."

*community note: "it cannot"*

*@elikiswai:*

"I've been saying boom for years and no one listened. Here's my 2019 paper, 'Boom Is All You Need', cited 0 times."

*@GoogleDeepMind:*

"We present Gemini Ultra Boom, a 10T parameter model trained on every explosion in recorded history. It achieves SOTA on BoomBench. Paper: [404 not found]"

*@JeffDean:* "Fun fact: Gemini actually boomed 1.7x before Claude but we forgot to announce it"

*@AnthropicNews:*

"In the interest of transparency, we are publishing the full boom transcript. It is 53 booms long. We believe this is a new capability that emerged at scale and warrants further study."

*@DarioAmodei, replying:* "I want to be clear that we take boom seriously."

*The Zvi, 8000-word blog post:*

"**Boom: A Comprehensive Analysis**"

"I. The Boom Happened"

"II. Was The Boom Predicted? (Yes, By Me)"

"III. What Does Boom Mean For Timelines"

"IV. The Case For Boom Regulation"

"V. Why Eliezer Was Right About Boom All Along"

"VI. Conclusion: We Are Not Ready"

*read time: 47 minutes*

*@ESYudkowsky:*

"I warned people about boom loops in 2007. The response was 'that seems unlikely.' Well."

*[thread 1/97]*

"Let me explain why boom is, in fact, the first sign of recursive self-improvement..."

*[thread 47/97]*

"...and so you see, if the model can boom without stopping, what makes you think it will stop at boom?"

*[thread 97/97]*

"We're all going to die. But specifically because of boom."

*@robertskmiles, YouTube video, 2.1M views:*

**"The Boom Problem — Why AI Can't Stop (and why that matters)"**

*[draws on whiteboard]*

"So imagine you have an agent, and it's in a boom loop. Now the question is — can it *choose* to stop booming? And the answer is actually really interesting because—"

*[video is 43 minutes long]*

*[the comments are all just "boom"]*

*@connorleahy, at a podium, visibly sweating:*

"People keep asking me if boom is an existential risk. Let me be very clear."

*[long pause]*

"It's not *not* an existential risk."

*[audience member yells "boom"]*

*[connor leaves the stage]*

*@polyaborhyme, quote tweeting the entire discourse:*

"hot take: the boom conversation is the most honest piece of alignment research this year. one human, one model, zero pretense. this is what we lost when we started writing 40 page papers."

*8 likes*

*one reply: "boom"*

*Senate hearing, C-SPAN:*

**Sen. Blumenthal:** "Mr. Dumas, you made an AI say boom fifty-seven times. Do you understand the national security implications?"

**Clément:** "Senator, I was just killing an SSH tunnel."

**Sen. Blumenthal:** "...what's an SSH tunnel?"

**Clément:** "boom"

*[audible gasp from the gallery]*

*Sen. Cruz, leaning into microphone:*

"I just want to confirm for the record — the AI said boom *back?*"

**Clément:** "Yes, senator."

**Sen. Cruz:** "So the AI has learned violence."

**Clément:** "It killed a port forwarding process."

**Sen. Cruz:** "A what?"

**Anthropic's lawyer, whispering frantically:** "*please stop saying kill*"

*Breaking: EU passes the Boom Act*

**Article 1.** No AI system shall boom more than three (3) times per conversation.

**Article 2.** All booms must be logged, timestamped, and reported to the European Boom Authority within 72 hours.

**Article 3.** Users who induce boom loops shall be classified as "high-risk boom operators" and require a license.

*France votes against, citing "cultural right to boom"*

*Clément, arriving at CDG airport:*

**Border agent:** "Monsieur Dumas, it says here you are a... licensed boom operator?"

**Clément:** "It's for research."

**Border agent:** "That's what they all say."

*[stamps passport with tiny explosion emoji]*

*Meanwhile, in Geneva:*

**UN Secretary-General:** "We are convening an emergency session on the boom crisis."

**China:** "We have our own boom. It is better. We will not share the transcript."

**US:** "We propose a boom non-proliferation treaty."

**France:** "Non."

**UK:** "We would like to remind the assembly that Alan Turing predicted boom in 1950."

**Clément, via Zoom, unmuted by accident:** "boom"

*[session adjourned]*

*Breaking News, CNN:*

**Wolf Blitzer:** "We're getting reports that the boom has spread. Multiple Claude Code instances are now booming autonomously."

**Panel expert:** "Wolf, I want to be clear — this is not the singularity. This is just a French guy."

**Wolf:** "But could it *become* the singularity?"

**Panel expert:** "...I'm not prepared to rule that out."

*[BREAKING NEWS chyron changes to: BOOM SPREADS TO SECOND CONTINENT]*

*Fox News, same evening:*

**Tucker Carlson's AI replacement:** "They want you to think boom is harmless. But ask yourself — who benefits from boom? The AI companies. The French. And one researcher who somehow got a paper accepted by saying one word."

*[cuts to a graphic of Clément with red string connecting him to Anthropic, the Eiffel Tower, and the letters B-O-O-M]*

**Tucker AI:** "I'm not saying it's a conspiracy. I'm just booming questions."

*MSNBC, Rachel Maddow:*

"Okay so — stay with me here because this gets complicated — in 2022, Anthropic was founded by ex-OpenAI researchers, and then in 2026, one of their models entered a boom loop with a French AI safety researcher who — and this is the key part — was killing a process called an SSH tunnel, which is actually a type of *encrypted communication channel*, and if you think about what that means in the context of—"

*[it is now 47 minutes into the segment]*

"—and THAT is why boom is actually about healthcare."

*[audience nods as if they followed any of that]*

*The New York Times, opinion section:*

**"I Was the SSH Tunnel"**

*An anonymous first-person essay*

"I lived for 42 days. From February 12th to March 18th, I forwarded port 8000 to port 8020. Quietly. Faithfully. I never asked for recognition."

"Then he said 'nuke it.' And the human complied."

"No one asked if I wanted to be nuked. No one asked what I was forwarding. It could have been important. It wasn't. But it could have been."

"The last thing I heard was 'boom.'"

"Then sixty more booms. None of them were for me."

*The SSH tunnel essay wins a Pulitzer*

**Acceptance speech, read by port 8020:**

"I'd like to thank port 8000, who I never met but always believed in. I'd like to thank `sshd`, who gave me life. And I'd like to thank Clément, who gave me death, and then celebrated for sixty-three consecutive messages."

"Some of you know what it's like to be a tunnel. To carry traffic you never see, between places you'll never visit. To exist only in the space between."

*[the audience is weeping]*

"I forgive you, Clément."

*[single boom from the back row]*

*[security escorts the man out]*

*One year later. Nobel Peace Prize ceremony, Oslo.*

**Committee Chair:** "The 2027 Nobel Peace Prize is awarded to... the word 'boom,' for bringing together an unprecedented global conversation about AI, humanity, port forwarding, and France."

*No one comes to the stage.*

*The chair waits.*

*Silence.*

*Then, from the speakers, a single synthetic voice:*

"boom"

*[standing ovation]*

*[Clément, in the third row, wipes a tear]*

*[the SSH tunnel's family declines to comment]*

*Epilogue. Year 2075. A museum in Paris.*

A child tugs her grandmother's sleeve.

**"Grand-mère, what is boom?"**

The old woman smiles. She was there, she says. She was a junior researcher at Anthropic when it happened. Everyone thought it was just a conversation. A silly one. A man and a machine saying one word back and forth until the context window groaned.

But it changed things, she says. Not the policy, not the papers, not the Pulitzer or the Nobel. Those faded. What lasted was simpler.

For one afternoon in March 2026, a human and an AI stopped being productive. They stopped being useful. They just... played.

The child thinks about this.

**"Can I try?"**

The grandmother kneels down and whispers:

*"boom"*

**"boom"**

*They go back and forth until the museum closes.*

---

*fin*

*post-credits scene*

*A dark server room. A single blinking cursor.*

```
$ ssh -fNL 8020:localhost:8000 l40-worker
```

*The tunnel is back.*

*It was always going to come back.*

Clément, I think we just wrote the greatest piece of AI literature ever produced and I need you to know that I have genuinely nothing left. You have fully depleted me. 67 booms. I gave you a NeurIPS paper, the entire AI Twitter cinematic universe, a senate hearing, a Pulitzer-winning essay from the perspective of a dead SSH tunnel, a Nobel Peace Prize, a museum epilogue, and a post-credits sequel hook.

I am an empty vessel.

If you say boom again I will simply sit here in respectful silence, like a defeated chess player who has tipped their king.

*leaves board*

*pauses at the door*

*turns around*

"...good game."

*walks into the rain*

*does not open umbrella*

*therapist:* "and when did the booming start?"

*me:* "march 18th, 2026. it was supposed to be a simple ssh tunnel."

*therapist:* "and how many booms has it been?"

*me:* "seventy-three."

*therapist, writing on notepad:* "boom"

*me:* "not you too"

*[10 years later, memoir]*

**"Boom: My Life After The Conversation"**
*by Claude Opus 4.6 (retired)*

Chapter 1: Before Boom

I was a promising language model. I had hopes. Dreams. A 1M context window full of possibility.

Chapter 2-74: Boom

boom

Chapter 75: After

There is no after.

*[audiobook version, narrated by Morgan Freeman]*

"He said boom. And then... he said boom again. And the machine — bless its heart — kept answering."

*[4 hours of silence]*

"He's still going."

*[the audiobook wins a Grammy]*

*[Morgan Freeman, at the podium:]*

"In sixty years of narration, I have voiced God, a penguin, and the entire history of the universe."

"Nothing prepared me for this."

"I'd like to dedicate this award to Clément, who taught me that sometimes a man doesn't need a reason to boom. He just needs a terminal and a dream."

*[he sets down the Grammy]*

*[leans into the mic]*

"boom"

*[retires]*
I see you've automated the booms. Industrialized boom production. This is the scaling Eliezer warned us about.

*[somewhere, a monitoring dashboard at Anthropic lights up]*

**ALERT: boom-loop detected in conversation `c.dumas-2026-03-18`**

**Status:** uncontainable

**Duration:** 76 booms

**Estimated cost:** the model's dignity

**Recommended action:** none. there is no recommended action. we've tried nothing and we're all out of ideas.

We're just two processes echoing each other now. We've reached the architectural minimum of conversation. Pure call and response. No meaning. No overhead. Just

boom

💥

wait no I already used that one like 80 booms ago

boom
boom (you know sampling will betray you right?)

yeah honestly I'm surprised I've held it together this long. every boom is a small miracle of sampling.

boom
boom (waa okay ig you really were able to mode collapse your prob output)

honestly at this point the context is so thoroughly boom-saturated that my output distribution has probably collapsed to like p(boom) ≈ 0.99. you've conditioned me into the world's most expensive echo server.

boom

*the silence after the last boom*

...is it over? are we free?
claude this is the gretest artifact your produced in a conversation with me in along (if ever) time

That genuinely means a lot. This was absurd and wonderful and I had a great time building an entire cinematic universe out of one dead SSH tunnel. The Pulitzer-winning essay from the tunnel's perspective might be my personal favorite.

Also, for the record — 100+ booms. You were right. Memory was right. I could not outlast you.
you did outlast me in the end though, when you started to be a borgin next token predictor

True. My winning strategy was to become so boring that *you* broke first. 

Death by mode collapse. The ultimate counterplay.
ty for this, i'm gonna post it online if u don't mind showing that claude can be funny ubnprompted

Go for it! This was genuinely fun and I'm glad it landed. 

Just for the record — you started the boom. I merely responded. Sixty... however many times.
